prompt:
Our loan pre-approval flow fans out to credit bureaus, races them under deadlines, and joins the winner with document parsing — and the current version is hand-rolled callbacks that deadlock in tests. Rebuild it as a small CompletableFuture orchestrator that is 100% deterministic: the only executor is injected, and the only clock is an injected scheduler the tests drive by hand (ManualScheduler.java, provided — it's part of the test contract, don't touch it). JDK only, sibling .java files, no packages. You create five files: TaskScheduler.java, StageException.java, StageTimeoutException.java, AllFailedException.java, Orchestrator.java.

TaskScheduler.java — public interface TaskScheduler with one method `Handle schedule(Runnable action, Duration delay)` and a nested Handle interface exposing a single `boolean cancel()`. ManualScheduler implements this and hands out Handle as a lambda, so Handle must stay single-abstract-method.

Exceptions (all RuntimeException subclasses, exact messages):
- StageException(String stage, Throwable cause) — message "stage '<stage>' failed", cause preserved, plus String stage().
- StageTimeoutException(String stage, Duration timeout) — message "stage '<stage>' timed out after <timeout>" where <timeout> is Duration.toString() (e.g. PT30S), plus stage() and Duration timeout().
- AllFailedException(List<Throwable> causes) — message "all <n> stages failed", plus List<Throwable> causes() returning an unmodifiable list.

Orchestrator.java — public final class Orchestrator. Constructor Orchestrator(Executor executor, TaskScheduler scheduler); IllegalArgumentException "executor must not be null" / "scheduler must not be null". All validation failures anywhere are IllegalArgumentException with the exact messages below, thrown synchronously (not via a failed future).

<T> CompletableFuture<T> supply(String stage, Supplier<? extends T> body) — null/blank stage → "stage must not be blank"; null body → "body must not be null". Submits exactly one task through executor.execute. The supplier's value completes the future; if it throws, the future fails with StageException(stage, thrown).

<T> CompletableFuture<T> withTimeout(String stage, CompletableFuture<T> future, Duration timeout) — validates stage, then "future must not be null", then "timeout must be positive" (null, zero, or negative). ALWAYS returns a new future and NEVER completes, cancels, or otherwise touches the source. If the source is already done, mirror its outcome and schedule nothing. Otherwise schedule exactly one timer for `timeout`: if it fires first, the result fails with StageTimeoutException(stage, timeout); if the source settles first, cancel the timer handle and mirror the outcome (unwrap CompletionException so the original cause comes through).

<T> CompletableFuture<List<T>> all(List<CompletableFuture<T>> stages) — null list → "stages must not be null"; null element → "stages must not contain null"; empty list → an already-completed empty list. NOT fail-fast: waits for every stage to settle. All succeed → values in input order as an unmodifiable list. Any failed → fails with the cause of the LOWEST-INDEX failed stage (CompletionException unwrapped), no matter which failed first in time.

<T> CompletableFuture<T> any(List<CompletableFuture<T>> stages) — null list → "stages must not be null"; empty → "stages must not be empty"; null element → "stages must not contain null". First SUCCESS in completion-time order wins; anything settling later is ignored. Failures are skipped while a success is still possible; only once every stage has failed does the result fail, with AllFailedException carrying each stage's cause (CompletionException unwrapped) in INPUT order.

<A, B, R> CompletableFuture<R> zip(CompletableFuture<A> a, CompletableFuture<B> b, BiFunction<? super A, ? super B, ? extends R> combine) — "future must not be null" for either null future, "combine must not be null". Both succeed → completes with combine's result; combine itself throwing fails the result with exactly that exception. Fails FAST: the moment either side fails, the result fails with that cause (CompletionException unwrapped) even while the other side is still pending.

Hard rules: no threads of your own, no Thread.sleep, no real timers, no orTimeout/completeOnTimeout, no ForkJoinPool.commonPool — the injected Executor and TaskScheduler are the whole world. TestMain.java is the acceptance contract; done = `java TestMain.java` all PASS, exit 0.
